Executive Summary
The Days We Build

When the Church speaks of "Days to Never Be Forgotten," it is not describing a marketing theme. It is describing an outcome — a sacred memory that should live beyond a visit, beyond a single day, beyond Temple Square.

Those days are created in moments. A volunteer who knows exactly what to do, and does it with warmth. A leader who reinforces the same standard across every zone, every shift. A guest who feels emotionally, physically, and spiritually safe.

Our Conviction
A catalogue of eLearning modules alone cannot produce Days to Never Be Forgotten.

The Church needs an integrated Open House operating architecture — a model that defines how leaders lead, how volunteers serve, how standards are reinforced, how recovery is handled, and how the system improves week by week. That is what Temple Open House Operating Model 1.0 is.

Beyond the Magic Consulting LLC · Performance ArchitectsCONFIDENTIAL – NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION | Non-binding | © Beyond the Magic Consulting LLC
Our Approach
Architects of Consistent Results

Beyond the Magic is not approaching this as a vendor delivering training. We are approaching this as architects installing an operating model.

  • Establish a baseline — not opinions
  • Create a Common Purpose & Service Standards leaders can reinforce
  • Translate standards into observable, coachable behaviors
  • Embed behaviors into onboarding, communication & daily operations
  • Build practical tools volunteers can use on shift
  • Deploy fast, light, repeatable measurement
  • Provide on-site observational analysis to protect performance in real time
Structure Is the Strategy
Everything else is activity. We design the systems that make consistent performance predictable.
The Name Tag Framework
Diagnose
Map current state. Find the gap.
Design
Architect the system.
Deploy
Implement, measure, iterate.
Sustain
Observe, adjust, protect.

Engagement Structure
Four Phases. One Operating Model.

Each phase prevents the most common failure point in large-scale volunteer events — leadership alignment at the top, and variability at the front line.

Phase I
Build It
Kickoff  ·  Baseline  ·  Senior Alignment
Activation 1
Virtual Kickoff

Activation 2

Phase I · Build It
Event Planning Analysis & Senior Leader Alignment
Part A — May 11–12 · Event Planning Analysis
  • Systems discovery across all planning functions
  • Physical walkthrough of spaces, queues & transitions
  • Guest and volunteer touchpoint mapping
  • Needs & wants analysis — emotional and operational
  • Recovery planning scope — where failure would cost most
Baseline Deliverables
  • Touchpoint map with hotspots & risk flags
  • Operational & emotional experience narrative
  • Recovery moments register
  • Measurement framework starting point
Part B — May 13–14 · Senior Leader Alignment
  • Establish the Common Purpose statement
  • Define Service Standards framework
  • Confirm leadership reinforcement expectations
  • Align operational zone ownership map
  • Set success indicators & measurement approach

Phase II
Create It
Standards → Behaviors → Systems → Tools

Standards not translated into observable behaviors become inspirational posters. Behaviors not embedded into systems become optional.

Phase II · Create It
Work Teams, Content Development & Deliverables
Champion Team Workshop
On-Site · Jun 2–3, 2026

Frontline leaders translate Service Standards into observable, coachable behaviors.

Work Team Alpha
Virtual · Jun 9–Jul 2, 2026

SMEs from Onboarding, Facilities, Transport & Technology embed the model into systems.

Work Team Beta
Virtual · Jul 14–Aug 6, 2026

All 8 volunteer functions narrow the model into role-specific execution and tool requirements.

Phase III
Roll It Out
Leader Activation  ·  Then Volunteer Activation

If leaders are not trained to reinforce the standard, drift begins immediately. Three delivery options — one powerful outcome.

Phase III · Roll It Out
Three Leader Rollout Options · Oct 26–29, 2026
Option A · Direct Leader Rollout
Oct 27–29 · Up to 3 sessions / 50 per session
  • Teach Common Purpose, Service Standards & behaviors
  • Train leaders on reinforcement, recognition & recovery
  • Introduce leader daily briefings & huddle cadence
  • Practice hotspot handling and recovery consistency
  • Activate pocket guide system & escalation guidance
Outcome
Leaders run the model independently without external support.
Option B · Train the Trainer
Oct 26–29 · 4-day intensive
Day 1
Leadership Reinforcement
Operating model ownership & standards mastery
Day 3
Content Mastery
Facilitation up-skilling & content deep-dive
Day 4
Rehearsal & Teach-Back
Readiness confirmation before deployment
Outcome
Church can scale training for future Open Houses independently.
★ Recommended
Option C · Hybrid Model
Oct 26–29 · Combines A + B
1
2× half-day leadership training sessions (up to 50 each)
2
Leadership reinforcement & operating model ownership
3
Content mastery & facilitation up-skilling
4
Rehearsal & teach-back — confirmed readiness
Outcome
Leaders run independently and scale with consistency for future Open Houses.
Beyond the Magic Consulting LLC · Performance ArchitectsCONFIDENTIAL – NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION | Non-binding | © Beyond the Magic Consulting LLC
Phase III · Roll It Out
Volunteer Activation Through eLearning · January 2027

All volunteers complete the required JOY Team eLearning prior to activation. This module is intentionally high-impact and simple.

The Module Covers
  • What the JOY Team is and how the volunteer is part of it
  • What is expected — behavioral standards in plain language
  • How to deliver the standard in every interaction
  • How recognition works and why it matters
  • How to use pocket guides and who to contact for help
  • High-level safety messages and escalation guidance
Key Positioning
We are not relying on eLearning as the primary readiness mechanism. We are using it as a universal baseline that points every volunteer into tools and leader support.
Technical Specifications
  • SCORM-compliant — built with Church media team
  • All content finalized by September 30, 2026
  • Minimum 2 review cycles per module
  • Deployment via Church internal LMS
Beyond the Magic Consulting LLC · Performance ArchitectsCONFIDENTIAL – NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION | Non-binding | © Beyond the Magic Consulting LLC
Phase IV
Sustain It
Observational Analysis  ·  Volunteer Induction  ·  Weekly Dashboards

The greatest risk to a sacred experience at scale is not planning — it is drift. Phase IV protects the experience in real time.

Measurement & Evaluation
Fast. Light. Repeatable. Actionable.

Three surveys — completed in under 3 minutes, mobile-optimized, randomized to avoid fatigue. Results drive weekly dashboards with course corrections for each wave.

Volunteer Survey
Confidence & Readiness
Weekly · 40% random sample across all 8 zones

Preparedness · Role clarity · Training relevance · Wave-by-wave comparison
Leader / Staff Survey
Standards Reinforcement
Monthly · 20% random sample of leadership

Standards alignment · Operational effectiveness · Reinforcement cadence
Guest Experience Survey
The Visitor Lens
Weekly · 40% via QR or email

Volunteer warmth · Helpfulness · Clarity · Overall guest experience
Post-Event Recommendations
Strategic recommendations for replicating this model at future Church events — delivered at the November 2027 Executive Recap Summit as a formal operating model handoff package.

Meeting the Intent. Strengthening the Outcome.
RFP RequirementBTM ResponseStatus
23 role-specific eLearning modules1 JOY Team eLearning + pocket guides + 20–25 role trainer guides. Reduces completion risk; increases on-shift utility.Met / Enhanced
8 zone facilitator guidesZone-based multi-page guides, simple checklist style, aligned to job aids for each zone.Met
20–25 role job aids (2 pg max)Print-ready PDF files per role/sub-role, updateable throughout the event.Met
In-person training supportFacilitation materials, zone guides, shift leader reference tools, ILT best practice recommendations.Met
Secret shopper evaluations (3 waves)Observational Event Planning Analysis by BTM personnel throughout all event waves — expanded scope.Met / Expanded
Volunteer & guest surveysDigital surveys, randomized sampling, wave-by-wave analysis, actionable recommendations and reports.Met
Mid-event training adjustmentsJust-in-time print materials, eLearning updates for subsequent waves, change order process included.Met
Future event replicationNovember 2027 Executive Recap Summit includes full operating model handoff package.Met
SCORM compliance & review cyclesAll assets SCORM-compliant. 2+ review cycles per module in partnership with Church media team.Met

Investment
Engagement Pricing & Options
Important — Please Read Before Reviewing Pricing

This financial investment summary is based upon the current scoping of your engagement. Changes to the scope and/or length will affect pricing. Pricing subject to change until signed contract is received. All pricing is in USD (U.S. Dollars).

Guarantee. No promise, guaranty, representation or warranty of any kind or nature whatsoever (including, without limitation, as to the implementation of any learning from any activity or service) is or will be made as to the success or failure of any activity or service on your or any other individual's or entity's business or operations.

Stewardship Framing
Volunteer Lens
~$20–$30 per volunteer to ensure they are inducted, prepared, supported, and consistent in delivering a sacred, guest-facing experience.
Visitor Lens
~$0.15–$0.30 per visitor across an estimated 3–5 million guests — protecting the integrity of the Open House and the long-term perception it creates.

Investment Options
Three Paths. One Operating Model.
Option 1
Foundation Architecture
$700,000
Phases I–III in full. Phase IV with monthly EPA, weekly dashboards. Volunteer Induction in February only. Best fit when the Church wants the Operating Model installed with leaner induction support post-readiness.
★ Recommended
Option 2
Operating Model 1.0 with Launch Protection
$825,000
Full Phase IV sustainment as designed. VIS in February, May, and July. Expanded 4-day on-site in March and April. Best fit for minimizing drift and ensuring consistent readiness at every wave.
Option 3
Legacy & Replication Model
$900,000
Everything in Option 2. Adds September VIS as included. Enhances November Summit focus on replication for future Open Houses. Best fit when the Church wants to institutionalize the model.
All options include Phases I, II & III in full  ·  All on-site trips  ·  All virtual work sessions  ·  Content final by Sep 30, 2026  ·  Airfare, lodging & meals for BTM team  ·  Complete digital files with rights transferred

About Us
Beyond the Magic Consulting LLC

Beyond the Magic is a performance architecture firm. We do not train people. We design the systems that make consistent performance predictable.

Most organizations invest in training and culture while the underlying system architecture remains unchanged. We address the root cause directly — through deliberate system design.

Our Philosophy
Structure is the strategy. Everything else is activity.
